text:
In sleepy towns where reality blends with dreams, Daisy lived upstairs. One moonlit night, she noticed owlhoots staring ominously through her window. Afraid.

"Poor owlhoots." Perhaps their owling couldn't keep them awake. She leaned out. They screeched.

Just then, lights flickered. Her friends? Daisy tiptoed downstairs.

Inside sat Old Harper. He sighed. Last fox hunting trip vanished.

"We won't bother ya." Daisy.

Harper chuckled.

"Why owlhoots?"

"We reckon." Hawks. They flew south. Owls...maybe?

"Don't think." Owls. She clucked.

Next dawn.

Daisy strolled.

Owlhoots vanished.

Sleep Tight.


I think it sounds lovely.
That's ok.
